title: Darth Millennial
content:

{{Quote|It was known by the ancients that a Dark Lord with three eyes has a secret strength possessed by none other.|Trioculus|<ref name="GODV">''[[The Glove of Darth Vader]]''</ref>}}
'''Darth Millennial''' was a three-eyed [[mutant]] [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] and [[Shadow Hand]], heir to the lineage of [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]], who lived almost a [[1000 BBY/Legends|thousand years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Apprenticed to [[Darth Cognus]], Millennial was powerful in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and had the ability to see the future. However, he and his [[Sith Master/Legends|Master]] disagreed on many aspects about the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], and Millennial became disillusioned with the [[Rule of Two/Legends|Rule of Two]], which he felt was far too restrictive, and saw far more sense in [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Skere Kaan|Kaan's]] [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Rule by the Strong]].

Eventually, he left his enraged Master and founded the [[Dark Force]] religion, which would later become the [[Prophets of the Dark Side]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]]. Proclaiming himself as a prophet chosen by the will of the Force, he attracted many [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-users]] from across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] to his religion before passing away.

==Biography==
===Life===
Darth Millennial was a three-[[Eye/Legends|eyed]] [[mutant]]<ref name="END" /> of [[Human/Legends|Human]] descent<ref name="CSWE" /> who lived almost [[1000 BBY/Legends|one thousand]] years before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].<ref name="CSWE2">{{CSWECite|II|313|Millennial, Darth}}</ref> He was at some point in his life seduced by the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], whereupon he joined the [[Order of the Sith Lords]]. He was apprenticed to a<ref name="END" /> [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Iktotchi/Legends|Iktotchi]]<ref name="DB3">''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]''</ref> [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], [[Darth Cognus]], who was a firm believer in [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane's]] [[Rule of Two/Legends|Rule of Two]], which stated that there should always be two [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]: a [[Sith Master/Legends|Master]] and an [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]]. Millennial proved to be a talented user of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]; he had powerful precognitive abilities and was trained as a [[Shadow Hand]] of Darth Cognus.<ref name="END" />

[[File:Darthcog.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Darth Cognus, Millennial's master]]
But while Millennial was a talented Force-user, he and Cognus disagreed on many aspects of the Sith,<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> most notably the Rule of Two. Millennial was forever questioning what he had been taught, and he vehemently disagreed with the philosophy of the Rule of Two,<ref name="END" /> which the mutant found to be far too restrictive.<ref name="Wizards">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050609a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 5: Two Peas in a Pod}}</ref> Instead, he believed in the more martial philosophy put forward by former Dark Lord and the founder of the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Skere Kaan|Kaan]]: the Rule by the Strong, which stated that there should be many Sith, with the leader being whomever was the strongest at any one time. Millennial's Master, though, rejected the concept, and the two clashed many times during his apprenticeship. Eventually, tensions reached a boiling point, and Millennial abandoned his Master and fled to the remote [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]], where he sought refuge from his Master's wrath.<ref name="END" /> Cognus trained a new apprentice and continued the lineage of the Rule of Two, while Millennial went about founding his own dark side religion on Dromund Kaas.<ref name="JvS" />

Millennial settled on the swamp world and spent much time contemplating and focusing on Kaan's ideas. Melding them with teachings of two [[Pre-Republic era]] philosophers named [[Plaristes]] and [[Dak Ramis]], Millennial started his own unique religion, named the [[Dark Force]], and inhabited a [[Dark Force Temple|temple]] in the swamps of Dromund Kaas. He proclaimed himself a prophet chosen by the will of the Force and invited [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-users]] from across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] to join him on Dromund Kaas and initiate themselves into the Dark Force. Many Force-sensitives from all sorts of backgrounds, as well as hordes of naïve Sith cultists, joined him, though they attempted to keep the religion secret. Anyone who did not believe in the Dark Force was slandered and labeled as a heretic, and Millennial and his religion encouraged the killing of such non-believers. Millennial became the [[Supreme Prophet]] of the Dark Side, and his organization made multitudes of prophecies during his lifetime.<ref name="END" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|Its next round of rediscovery came by someone who rejected the Rule of Two, who thought that there was more than enough of the dark side to go around. He founded a religion that would become known as the Dark Force. There's an ancient temple there still. It was serviced for centuries by the Dark Prophets. It's so steeped in the dark side that it formed its own nexus-one so powerful it interferes with all weaponry and technology except for lightsabers.|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]|<ref name="Ascension">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Ascension]]''</ref>}}
Eventually, Millennial passed away, but his religion lived on. Practitioners of the Dark Force came to be known as [[Prophets of the Dark Side]], and the order survived on Dromund Kaas in seclusion for centuries, away from the prying eyes of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Eventually, the Prophets came to the attention of a Sith Lord named [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], who recruited them as advisers in his capacity as [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] and [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]]. After the Prophets were attacked by Sidious's [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitors]], they fled to [[Bosthirda/Legends|Bosthirda]]. Millennial's order was eventually wiped out by a trio of [[darksider]]s: [[Azrakel]], a former student of the Prophets; [[Lumiya]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lady of the Sith]]; and her apprentice, [[Carnor Jax]].<ref name="END" /> By the end of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], they had yet to be reformed, at least not openly.<ref name="JvS" /> A [[Church of the Dark Side|false group of prophets]] sprung up after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], using the ancient prophecy that another three-eyed mutant would rise among the prophets to promote a [[Slavery/Legends|slave lord]] named [[Trioculus]] as the new Emperor.<ref name="GODV" /> These false prophets were themselves killed off, however.<ref name="WIW">{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}}</ref>

Millennial's existence was known to the [[New Jedi Order]],<ref name="Ascension" /> and, after the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] invasion, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Tionne Solusar]] published a series of texts, several of which mentioned Millennial. The New Jedi Order, however, knew little information other than that he founded the Dark Force.<ref name="JvS" /><ref name="Ascension" />

==Personality and traits==
Millennial was not one to blindly follow his superiors and questioned everything he was taught, formulating his own opinions. He saw strength in numbers and felt that the Sith would thrive under the Rule by the Strong. He felt that a leader should be the strongest of the group and that if another became stronger than the leader, he or she had the right to claim command for himself or herself. Millennial himself was a strong leader, as he persuaded many followers of the dark side to leave their respective orders and join him on Dromund Kaas.<ref name="END" />

Millennial had great belief in himself and his abilities, particularly those that allowed him to see the future, and he thought that he was special enough to be chosen as the prophet of the will of the Force.<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|abelgpena|74|Extinct for Over a Millennium: or, A Darth By Any Other Name, Part 2}}</ref> But while Millennial was supremely confident in himself, he knew when to run and hide. After his and his Master's confrontations over how the Sith should be ruled escalated, he knew he was no match for her and went into hiding.<ref name="JvS" /> Millennial was pale-skinned, with a black-and-silver-colored [[Hair/Legends|beard]]. He had two blue eyes and an yellow-colored third eye that was positioned in the center of his forehead.<ref name="CSWE" />

==Powers and abilities==
Millennial had powerful precognitive abilities and was trained as a Shadow Hand of Darth Cognus. Later, Millennial created his own organization of Force wielders that also had strong precognitive abilities.<ref name="END" />

==Behind the scenes==
Darth Millennial was created by [[Abel G. Peña]] prior to [[2001]], as part of ''[[Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties]]'', an article discussing the various lesser-known Sith organizations. The article describes Millennial as a "three-eyed mutant," [[Retroactive continuity|retconning]] him to be the Dark Lord mentioned in the children's novel ''[[The Glove of Darth Vader]]''. Peña considered the character to likely be Human, though this wasn't established firmly in [[canon]]. However, ''Evil Never Dies'' would not be published for a number of years; in the interim, Peña mentioned Millennial briefly in ''[[The Dark Forces Saga]]'', released on the [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Wizards.com|web site]] in [[June]] [[2005]], marking the character's first appearance in canon. ''Evil Never Dies'' was subsequently released on [[StarWars.com]] [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] [[2006|one year later]], expanding Millennial's backstory as established in ''The Dark Forces Saga''.<ref name="Blog" />

With Peña's previous Darth creation, [[Darth Ruin/Legends|Darth Ruin]], he had followed [[George Lucas]]'s pattern of short Sith names, such as [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] and [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]]; however, with Millennial, he decided to be adventurous and go with a longer name, in the same vein as Lucas' [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] and [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Darth Plagueis]]. Millennial was a religious figure, so Peña wanted to integrate the concept of infinity with his name; he chose to base it off the word "millennium," meaning a thousand years, as one thousand is the [[Wikipedia:Arabic|Arabic]] symbol used to represent infinity, similar to how Western Culture uses the ∞ symbol, and [[Wikipedia:Chinese culture|Chinese culture]] the 萬物. The figure one thousand also crops up in a number of prominent ''[[Star Wars]]'' works, such as [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']], [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''The Phantom Menace'']], and [[Tom Veitch]]'s ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'', which contributed toward Peña's choice. Additionally, as Millennial existed around the same time the Sith became extinct, Peña sees his name as a prophecy in itself, as the Sith remained extinct for a thousand years.<ref name="Blog" />

Millennial was subsequently referenced in [[Ryder Windham]]'s [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']], as well as [[Matthew Grau]]'s [[Behind the Threat: The Sith|''Behind the Threat: The Sith, Part 1: Portrayal'']], and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', by [[Daniel Wallace]], [[Pablo Hidalgo]], [[Stephen J. Sansweet]], and [[Bob Vitas]]. In ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'', Millennial's name is misspelled as "Darth Millenial." He also received his first official depiction in that work, which showed him to be Human, canonizing Peña's previous comments that he was likely a member of that [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="Blog" />
